hypothesis: If a stereochemistry-aware diffusion model with an equivariant DiT+EGNN architecture trained on a massive QM-optimized conformer dataset (LoQI with explicit E/Z and R/S graph encodings) is used to generate conformers for unseen drug-like molecules, then it will produce topology- and stereochemistry-correct structures with near-DFT low energies (often lower than existing references) requiring minimal relaxation, because the model learns physically consistent 3D geometry and energetics from stereochemically augmented training data.
